data.reasoningText:
The project is a .NET 10 console app with some packages already referenced:
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI` - for AI agent capabilities
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` - for OpenAI integration via Microsoft.Extensions.AI
- `OpenAI` - OpenAI SDK

I need to build out a research agent that:
1. Uses an AI chat client (via Microsoft.Extensions.AI)
2. Has a web search tool
3. Has a note-taking tool
4. Researches a topic and summarizes findings

Let me use `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` which provides the `IChatClient` abstraction and tool-calling support. 

I can define tools using the `AIFunction` attribute on static methods, then create an `IChatClient` with those tools registered in `ChatOptions`. The agent loop will let the AI invoke these tools as needed to research and summarize the topic. ChatClient` with function invocation middleware to handle tool calls automatically. I'll create two tools—one for web searches and another for note-taking—then wire them into the agent loop so it can call them as needed during the conversation. Building the chat client... Writing the agent implementation... Setting up the system prompt to guide the research agent through a structured process of searching, taking notes, and synthesizing findings, then executing the chat client to get the research response and display the summary. After that, I'm moving into helper functions to prompt the user for their research topic.

Now I'm reconsidering how to properly instantiate the AI functions from the tools class—the factory method approach needs adjustment to work correctly with instance methods. I'm uncertain about the exact API shape for `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` 9.x, so the most practical approach is to write the implementation and then iterate based on build errors. Let me check whether it's `GetResponseAsync` returning `ChatResponse` or an earlier preview API, and what the function invocation middleware looks like. I'll skip the restore approach and just write the implementation directly, then build and fix any issues as they come up. Setting up the configuration with the topic, API key, and model selection, then starting to write out the Program.cs file with the agent implementation...

Writing the agent code... Defining the note-taking tools...

Now I'm setting up the chat client with function invocation enabled, and initializing the conversation history with a system prompt that instructs the agent to research a topic through multiple web searches, take notes on findings, review them, and provide a comprehensive summary. I'm checking whether the `AIFunctionFactory.Create` overload in the 9.x API supports passing a lambda with name and description parameters, and considering whether C# attributes work on lambda parameters for additional metadata. I'm checking the exact API surface for `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` 9.x — whether it's `AsIChatClient()` or `AsChatClient()`, and how to properly instantiate `ChatClientBuilder`. Rather than getting bogged down in version details, I'll just write the implementation and iterate if needed. I also need to verify the correct `ChatRole` constant for system messages. Writing the implementation...

   - `IChatClient` interface - method names (is it `GetResponseAsync` or `CompleteAsync`?)

4. **Microsoft.Agents.AI** (v1.3.0) - Check under `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/` for:

</member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools">
            <summary>Gets or sets the list of tools to include with a chat request.</summary>
            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#tool-calling">Tool calling.</related>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
            <summary>Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
--
            <para>
            The clone will have the same values for all properties as the original instance. Any collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/>,
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.StopSequences"/>, and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AdditionalProperties"/>, are shallow-cloned, meaning a new collection instance is created,
            but any references contained by the collections are shared with the original.
            </para>
            <para>
            Derived types should override <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Clone"/> to return an instance of the derived type.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
--
            <remarks>
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> can contain zero or more <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/>
            instances, and the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> is free to invoke zero or more of them.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatToolMode.None">
            <summary>
            Gets a predefined <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatToolMode"/> indicating that tool usage is unsupported.

            <summary>

        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.LoggingChatClient">
            <summary>A delegating chat client that logs chat operations to an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger"/>.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The provided implementation of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> is thread-safe for concurrent use so long as the
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger"/> employed is also thread-safe for concurrent use.
            </para>
            <para>
            When the employed <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger"/> enables <see cref="F:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Trace"/>, the contents of
            chat messages and options are logged. These messages and options may contain sensitive application data.

<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides extensions for <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentExtensions.AsBuilder(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent)">
            <summary>
            Creates a new <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder"/> using the specified agent as the foundation for the builder pipeline.
            </summary>
            <param name="innerAgent">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> instance to use as the inner agent.</param>
            <returns>A new <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder"/> instance configured with the specified inner agent.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="innerAgent"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            This method provides a convenient way to convert an existing <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> instance into
            a builder pattern, enabling easily wrapping the agent in layers of additional functionality.
            It is functionally equivalent to using the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent)"/> constructor directly,
            but provides a more fluent API when working with existing agent instances.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentExtensions.AsAIFunction(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession)">
            <summary>
            Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> that runs the provided <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>.
            </summary>
            <param name="agent">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> to be represented as an invocable function.</param>
            <param name="options">
            Optional metadata to customize the function representation, such as name and description.
            If not provided, defaults will be inferred from the agent's properties.
            </param>
            <param name="session">
            Optional <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> to use for function invocations. If not provided, a new session
            will be created for each function call, which may not preserve conversation context.
            </param>
            <returns>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> that can be used as a tool by other agents or AI models to invoke this agent.
            </returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="agent"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This extension method enables agents to participate in function calling scenarios, where they can be
            invoked as tools by other agents or AI models. The resulting function accepts a query string as input and
            returns the agent's response as a string, making it compatible with standard function calling interfaces
            used by AI models.
            </para>
            <para>
--

            <summary>

            </summary>
        </member>

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

# Parse the XML file
tree = ET.parse('/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.3.0/lib/net9.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions.xml')
root = tree.getroot()

# Find AIAgent and related types
ns = {'doc': 'http://docs.microsoft.com/xml/doc/api'}
for member in root.findall('.//member[@name]'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    if 'AIAgent' in name or 'AgentSession' in name:
        summary = member.find('summary')
        if summary is not None and summary.text:
            print(f"{name}: {summary.text.strip()}")

EOF

# Parse the XML file
tree = ET.parse('/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.3.0/lib/net9.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions.xml')
root = tree.getroot()

# Find AIAgent and related types
ns = {'doc': 'http://docs.microsoft.com/xml/doc/api'}
for member in root.findall('.//member[@name]'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    if 'AIAgent' in name or 'AgentSession' in name:
        summary = member.find('summary')
        if summary is not None and summary.text:
            print(f"{name}: {summary.text.strip()}")

EOF

toolArgs.prompt:
I need to understand the APIs available from these NuGet packages for building an AI agent with tool calling in .NET 10:

1. **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (v9.10.2) - Check the XML docs at `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/9.10.2/lib/net9.0/Microsoft.Extensions.AI.xml` for:
   - `ChatClientBuilder` and its methods (especially `UseFunctionInvocation`)
   - `AIFunctionFactory.Create` overloads
   - `AIFunction` class
   - `ChatOptions` and its `Tools` property type

2. **Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions** - Find the XML docs under `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/` and check:
   - `IChatClient` interface - method names (is it `GetResponseAsync` or `CompleteAsync`?)
   - `ChatMessage` constructors and `ChatRole` constants
   - `ChatResponse` or `ChatCompletion` return types
   - `AITool` base class

3. **Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI** (v10.3.0) - Check under `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/10.3.0/` for:
   - Extension method name to convert OpenAI ChatClient to IChatClient (is it `AsIChatClient()` or `AsChatClient()`?)

4. **Microsoft.Agents.AI** (v1.3.0) - Check under `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/` for:
   - What does this package provide? Any agent loop abstractions?

Report the exact class names, method signatures, and API patterns I should use.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the API details I need. Let me also check if `ChatClientAgent` exists in the Microsoft.Agents.AI package, since it was mentioned in the exploration.

Key findings:
- `IChatClient.GetResponseAsync()` returns `ChatResponse`
- `AsIChatClient()` is the extension method
- `ChatClientBuilder` with `UseFunctionInvocation()` for auto tool calling
- `AIFunctionFactory.Create(Delegate, name, description)` for creating tools
- `ChatRole.System`, `ChatRole.User`, etc.
- `ChatMessage(ChatRole, string)` constructor


- `Microsoft.Agents.AI` contains `AIAgent`, `AIAgentBuilder`, and `ChatClientAgent` among other agent implementations

I should verify whether `ChatClientAgent` is actually a concrete class in the package or if it was inferred from the exploration, since I want to make sure I'm working with real APIs.

data.content: Now I have a clear picture of the APIs. Let me write the implementation using `ChatClientAgent` from `Microsoft.Agents.AI`, which provides a proper agent loop with automatic tool invocation.
data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the info I need. Let me write the implementation using `ChatClientAgent` from `Microsoft.Agents.AI`:

Key API:
- `ChatClientAgent` constructor: `(IChatClient, string instructions, string? name, string? description, IList<AITool>? tools, ...)`
- `agent.RunAsync(string message, AgentSession? session)` - session can be null and a new one is created
- `AgentResponse.Text` - gets the text response
- `AgentResponse.ToString()` - also works
- `ChatClientAgent` automatically handles function invocation (from the docs: "By default the ChatClientAgent applies decorators to the provided IChatClient for doing for example automatic function invocation")


- The agent handles the tool-calling loop automatically, so I just need to set up the client, define the tools, instantiate the agent, and call `RunAsync` with the user message

Writing the implementation...

data.content:
The app builds cleanly. Here's what it does:

**Architecture:** Uses `ChatClientAgent` from `Microsoft.Agents.AI` which provides a full agent loop with automatic tool invocation on top of any `IChatClient`.

**Three tools registered:**
| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `WebSearch` | Searches the web for a query (placeholder — swap in Bing/Google API) |
| `TakeNote` | Saves a finding to an in-memory notebook |
| `ReadNotes` | Retrieves all saved notes for review |

**Agent workflow:** The system prompt instructs the agent to search 2–3 times with different angles, take notes after each search, review notes, then produce a structured summary.

**To run:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
dotnet run -- "quantum computing"
# or: OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o dotnet run -- "climate change solutions"
```

The `WebSearch` tool returns placeholder data — replace the lambda body with a real search API call (e.g., Bing Search REST API) for production use.
